01-book-argument-spine.excalidraw.title: The Argument Spine
01-book-argument-spine.excalidraw.caption: The ten chapters as a four-act dependency arc — the Problem (ch 1–2), the Scaffolding Stack (ch 3–7), the Stress Test (ch 8), and the Widening (ch 9–10). The book is an argument with a shape, not a survey, and it carries the throughline from possibility to compounding trust.

02-autoresearch-machine.excalidraw.title: The Autoresearch Knowledge Machine
02-autoresearch-machine.excalidraw.caption: How a 693-video corpus becomes a source-backed book: a five-layer pipeline — Source → Synthesis → Evidence → Manuscript — governed by a Research-Org control plane that improves the upper layers through bounded, logged agent passes.

03-scaffolding-stack.excalidraw.title: The Scaffolding Stack
03-scaffolding-stack.excalidraw.caption: The book's central thesis in one picture: a raw model is capable but not dependable. Five engineered layers wrap it — harnesses, evals, context, runtimes, security — and each one earns its place by a specific failure it prevents.

04-theme-corpus-map.excalidraw.title: Theme & Corpus Map
04-theme-corpus-map.excalidraw.caption: What 693 videos are actually about: ten themes sized by corpus count, colour-coded by the book act they feed, each mapped to its chapter — including the honest editorial call that Models & Inference is deliberately background, not a chapter.

08-chapter4-evals.excalidraw.title: Vibes & benchmarks vs operational eval loop

09-chapter5-context.excalidraw.title: Stuffing the window vs assembling context

01-agentic-product-design.excalidraw.title: Agentic Product Design
01-agentic-product-design.excalidraw.summary: Designing the surface area an agent acts through — intake, action, review — not just the model prompt.

02-agent-observability.excalidraw.title: Agent Observability
02-agent-observability.excalidraw.summary: Traces, metrics, and replay tooling that turn agent runs into something you can debug and improve.

03-agent-runtime-replay-vs-snapshot.exca.title: Replay vs Snapshot
03-agent-runtime-replay-vs-snapshot.exca.summary: Two strategies for durable agent state: reconstruct from event history vs preserve as checkpoints.

04-ai-native-organization.excalidraw.title: AI-Native Organization
04-ai-native-organization.excalidraw.summary: An operating model where AI is integrated into how work is created and reviewed, not just bolted on.

05-coding-evals.excalidraw.title: Coding Evals
05-coding-evals.excalidraw.summary: Task-level scoring for code-writing agents — human-seeded cases, regression memory, production traces.

06-constrained-delegation.excalidraw.title: Constrained Delegation
06-constrained-delegation.excalidraw.summary: Bounded autonomy — scoped identity, sandboxing, audit trail — that lets an agent act safely.

07-context-engineering.excalidraw.title: Context Engineering
07-context-engineering.excalidraw.summary: Deciding what the model should know at the moment of action — retrieval, memory, tool selection.

08-durable-agents.excalidraw.title: Durable Agents
08-durable-agents.excalidraw.summary: Agents with state, resumability, and recovery paths — architecture that survives a crash.

09-graphrag.excalidraw.title: GraphRAG
09-graphrag.excalidraw.summary: Retrieval over a graph rather than a flat vector index — useful when relationships matter as much as content.

10-harness-engineering.excalidraw.title: Harness Engineering
10-harness-engineering.excalidraw.summary: A prepared environment around an agent — specs, tests, tools, a plan → produce → review → ship loop.

11-hierarchical-memory.excalidraw.title: Hierarchical Memory
11-hierarchical-memory.excalidraw.summary: Layered memory — session, project, long-term — scoped, inspectable, maintained like infrastructure.

12-human-control-plane.excalidraw.title: Human Control Plane
12-human-control-plane.excalidraw.summary: Surfaces humans need to approve, intervene, audit, and escalate agent work.

13-model-context-protocol.excalidraw.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP)
13-model-context-protocol.excalidraw.summary: A protocol for connecting models to tools and data sources in a standard, inspectable way.

14-one-shot-ai-failure.excalidraw.title: One-shot AI Failure
14-one-shot-ai-failure.excalidraw.summary: The failure mode where an agent is asked to do everything in a single pass and silently produces slop.

15-software-factory.excalidraw.title: Software Factory
15-software-factory.excalidraw.summary: Work intake, constraints, review, and output paths — the surrounding system, not just the model prompt.

16-spec-driven-development.excalidraw.title: Spec-Driven Development
16-spec-driven-development.excalidraw.summary: Specs as executable acceptance criteria and repeatable checks — not paperwork.

17-vibe-coding.excalidraw.title: Vibe Coding
17-vibe-coding.excalidraw.summary: Shipping whatever runs — fast generation without review discipline. Useful sometimes, dangerous often.

18-voice-agents.excalidraw.title: Voice Agents
18-voice-agents.excalidraw.summary: Sub-800 ms latency budget, streaming, turn detection, barge-in — what realtime stress-tests demand.

ch01-fig3-chat-is-the-tip.excalidraw.title: Chat is the tip of the iceberg

01-readers-path.excalidraw.title: Reader's path
01-readers-path.excalidraw.caption: A suggested path through the chapters for different reader profiles.

02-methodology-deep-dive.excalidraw.title: Methodology deep-dive
02-methodology-deep-dive.excalidraw.caption: For readers who want to inspect The Method itself.
